anti_claim: This standard governs public body-free circuit-attribution runtime receipts and target refs. It does not authorize model-transparency product claims, private weight export, raw activation export, proprietary prompt export, hidden chain-of-thought export, provider calls, benchmark scores, publication, or release.

allowed_public_inputs: machine-readable attribution graph nodes and edges

receipt_expectations: receipt must cite feature counts, replay counts, attribution-edge counts, selected pattern ids, negative-case coverage, secret-exclusion scan, body-import verification, target refs, authority ceiling, and anti-claim

standard_payload.anti_purpose: A pretty circuit graph, feature-name story, or provider transcript that claims model transparency without copied public-safe attribution machinery, causal intervention receipts, failure limits, and secret exclusion.
failure_modes: private model weights or raw activation tensors copied into public fixtures
proprietary prompt bodies, hidden chain-of-thought, or provider payloads treated as inspectable circuit evidence
screenshot-only attribution graph accepted without machine-readable nodes and edges
feature labels asserted without catalog ids, visualization summary refs, or validation anchors
transparency or faithfulness claim made without causal intervention receipts and sufficiency limits
synthetic attribution fixture counted as macro body instead of being wrapped by exact copied source modules or named blocked-import debt
Oracle attribution-map prompt body or runtime receipt treated as live provider execution, benchmark result, or product transparency authority
standard_payload.ontology: A public-safe circuit-attribution replay organ that combines body-free replay rows with exact copied non-secret attribution-map source nodes, explicit causal-intervention refs, and failure-mode boundaries before any interpretability claim is allowed.
standard_payload.teleology: Make interpretability evidence falsifiable and source-open without leaking private model internals, provider payloads, prompt bodies, hidden reasoning, or benchmark/product authority.

validation_rules: require every replay row to cite toy prompt refs, sparse feature ids, machine-readable graph nodes and edges, replacement-model approximation scores, feature visualization summary refs, causal inhibition and injection deltas, sufficiency labels, faithfulness limits, contradiction cases, cold replay refs, and public runtime target refs

reject private model weights, raw activation dumps, proprietary prompts, hidden chain-of-thought, screenshot-only graphs, unverifiable feature names, transparency claims without causal intervention receipts, faithfulness claims without limits, benchmark claims, and release authority
require public runtime receipt authority, body-free public fixture rows, body-import verification, and secret-exclusion scan
